summary.authoritative_rule: until an explicit shadow-only extension program is opened, the lockout-aligned derivation boundary remains the authoritative stop and the shadow bridge stays blocked

triage_rows.rationale: the current lockout-aligned derivation boundary remains the authoritative default
existing raw coverage does not justify silently extending a frozen board-surface boundary
extension remains a possible future move, but only through an explicit shadow-lane policy shift
post-lockout unlock quality should not be debated before the boundary policy itself is changed
the bridge remains blocked while the derivation boundary stays frozen

interpretation: V1.34HE turns the boundary-policy audit into the current governance direction.
The next meaningful move is now policy-level, not signal-level: either keep the lockout-aligned boundary frozen or later open an explicit shadow-only extension program.
